2008-05-01.title: Yossi Harel, Who, Defying British, Brought Jews to Palestine, Is Dead at 90
2008-05-01.abstract: Mr. Harel renamed the rickety ship he commanded Exodus 1947 and sailed it to legend as a symbol of the righteousness of the mission by Jews to settle Palestine in the face of British opposition.

2008-05-01.title: In a New Climate Model, Short-Term Cooling in a Warmer World
2008-05-01.abstract: Climatologists will create decade-long climate forecasts, just as meteorologists craft weeklong weather forecasts.

2008-05-01.title: Pakistani Parties Weigh Reinstating Judges
2008-05-01.abstract: Pakistan’s governing coalition is holding talks over whether to restore the high court judges ousted by President Pervez Musharraf in November.

2008-05-01.title: Attacks in Pakistan Rising, State Department Reports
2008-05-01.abstract: Terrorist attacks against noncombatants more than doubled in Pakistan from 2006 to 2007, in part reflecting the growing violence in the country’s turbulent tribal areas .

2008-05-01.title: Afghans See Link to Qaeda in Plot to Shoot Karzai
2008-05-01.abstract: The Afghan intelligence chief said the attempt to kill President Hamid Karzai was the work of militants who had infiltrated his country’s security forces.

2008-05-02.title: The Oud: An Iraqi Lute
2008-05-02.abstract: Dhia Jabbar plays a song in Baghdad as a helicopter flies overhed.

2008-05-02.title: Zimbabwe: Officials Begin Verifying Election Results
2008-05-02.abstract: A month after Zimbabwe’s disputed election, representatives of the presidential candidates finally met to begin verifying an outcome that has yet to be announced.

2008-05-02.title: Iran Protests to U.N. About Clinton Comments
2008-05-02.abstract: Iran lodged a protest at the U.N. over comments from Hillary Clinton that the U.S. would “totally obliterate” Iran if it staged a nuclear attack on Israel.

2008-05-02.title: Stores Hint at Change Under New Castro
2008-05-02.abstract: President Raúl Castro’s tinkering may be tiny compared to Cuba’s problems, but he is still shaking up the country.

2008-05-02.title: Germany: Mayhem on May Day
2008-05-02.abstract: Left-wing protesters erected barricades in the streets of Hamburg and burned police cars as part of a demonstration against a march by neo-Nazis.

2008-05-03.title: Battle in Helmand Province, Afghanistan
2008-05-03.abstract: While the Pentagon began planning to send more U.S. troops to Afghanistan next year, Taliban and U.S. marines exchanged fire.

2008-05-03.title: Israel Clears Army in 5 Gaza Deaths
2008-05-03.abstract: The Israeli military found on Friday that the deaths, which occurred during an army operation on Monday, was due to explosions caused by Palestinian ammunition.

2008-05-03.title: Big Powers Agree on New Bid for Iranian Nuclear Freeze
2008-05-03.abstract: The world’s major powers agreed in London on Friday to offer Iran modest new incentives to coax it to freeze important nuclear activities.

2008-05-03.title: Zimbabwe Calls for Runoff, but Opposition Challenges Vote Count
2008-05-03.abstract: The election commission announced that the opposition candidate had not won by enough to avoid a runoff vote.

2008-05-03.title: Philipp von Boeselager, Who Attempted an Assassination of Hitler, Dies at 90
2008-05-03.abstract: Mr. Boeselager is believed to have been the last surviving member of the circle of German Army officers who tried to assassinate Adolf Hitler with a briefcase bomb in 1944.

2008-05-04.title: Palestinians Seek Support on Borders

2008-05-04.abstract: Turkish educators are offering an alternative approach to religious schools that could reduce extremists’ influence.

2008-05-04.title: Zimbabwe Party Seeks to Verify Vote
2008-05-04.abstract: The leaders of Zimbabwe’s opposition party were making a last-ditch diplomatic push on Saturday to persuade the African Union and a bloc of southern African nations to insist on a verifiable vote count.

2008-05-04.title: Missiles Strike Sadr City, Damaging Hospital
2008-05-04.abstract: Missiles damaged a hospital and many of its ambulances in Baghdad. A group of children was also injured.

2008-05-05.title: A Turkmen Dismantles Reminders of Old Ruler
2008-05-05.abstract: The decision by the president to remove the 246-foot-tall, rocket ship-like monument to the country’s late ruler is the latest step in dismantling that personality cult.

2008-05-05.title: Clashes in Bolivia on Vote Over More Autonomy

2008-05-05.abstract: The tourism deal would increase the number of tourists allowed to visit the island from the mainland.

2008-05-05.abstract: The legacy of Boris N. Yeltsin, dead for a little over a year, oscillates between the glory of his founding of a Russia that emerged from the Soviet Union and criticism of his erratic style.

2008-05-06.abstract: The attacks came a day after two other servicemen were killed in a gun battle between Russian forces and as many as 30 insurgents in the Chechen village of Urus-Martan.

2008-05-06.abstract: Russia’s foreign ministry eased the visa process for English soccer fans traveling to Moscow for a big game, lessening tensions generated by the murder of Aleksander V. Litvinenko

2008-05-06.abstract: Overshadowed by thriving monarchies, Kuwaitis are wondering if democracy is living up to its promises.

2008-05-06.abstract: Elections to fill vacant legislative seats will be delayed by two months, to Aug. 18 from June 18, the Election Commission announced.

2008-05-06.abstract: Three people were killed and 12 injured when a fire broke out on a municipal bus during morning rush hour in Shanghai.

2008-05-07.abstract: Blackouts are just one of the problems caused by an explosion in Japan’s population of crows, which seems to compete with humans for space in this crowded nation.

2008-05-07.abstract: Bad roads and a lack of cooperation from the military government could hamper aid efforts.

2008-05-07.title: South Africa: Cabinet Appointment Requested
2008-05-07.abstract: The governing African National Congress has asked South Africa’s president, Thabo Mbeki, to appoint the party’s deputy president, Kgalema Motlanthe, to serve in Parliament and in Mr. Mbeki’s cabinet.

2008-05-08.abstract: London’s new mayor said that he was banning alcohol on the city’s public transportation system, effective next month, in an effort to “end the problem of drunken and intimidating behavior on the Tube.”

2008-05-08.abstract: The concert performed for Pope Benedict XVI on Wednesday could signal the end to the tense relationship between the Vatican and the Chinese government.

2008-05-08.abstract: The leaders of China and Japan pledged to make their nations partners instead of rivals as Chinese President Hu Jintao began a goodwill mission to Tokyo.

2008-05-08.abstract: The skirmish escalated tensions as the country remained mired in its worst political crisis since the 1975-1990 civil war.

2008-05-10.abstract: Blackwater’s government contract has been renewed and no charges have been brought in the United States against its guards.

2008-05-11.abstract: The move raised hopes for a political settlement after street battles that have killed at least 29 people.

2008-05-12.abstract: Three hundred miles south of Baghdad, Basra has been transformed by its own surge, now seven weeks old.

2008-05-14.abstract: Barely recovered from the imposition of a smoking ban this year, the French are bracing for the next novelty in their favorite bistros: a Breathalyzer.

2008-05-14.title: Spain: Water Shipped to Parched City

The shipments are part of a broad effort to alleviate the impact of the worst drought in the region of Catalonia in decades. Fountains and beach showers have been turned off in Barcelona, and residents have been told not to water their gardens. The government will charter ships through October if the drought does not end, a spokesman for the regional environment department said.

2008-05-14.abstract: With dismal poll ratings, Gordon Brown is drawing comparisons to the hapless John Major, Britain’s last Conservative prime minister, ousted in the Labor landslide of 1997.

2008-05-22.abstract: English soccer fans and bemused Russians celebrated in ahead of the Champions League final between Manchester United and Chelsea.

2008-05-22.abstract: The finding supports reassurances by China and the United States that the giant earthquake of May 12 resulted in no nuclear releases.

2008-05-23.abstract: Violence in Johannesburg has forced thousands of frightened, hungry immigrants from their homes.

2008-05-23.abstract: Ban Ki-moon had a firsthand look at some of the damage as a program of international aid gained momentum.

2008-05-23.abstract: A memorial dedicated to gay men and lesbians persecuted and killed under the Nazis will be inaugurated on May 27, the Lesbian and Gay Federation in Germany said.

2008-05-24.abstract: The ruling party in Georgia triumphed in parliamentary elections, official results showed, cementing President Mikheil Saakashvili as the pre-eminent political force.

2008-05-27.title: 2 American Soldiers Are Killed in Insurgent Attacks in Iraq
2008-05-27.abstract: The latest attacks demonstrate that guerrillas remain a lethal presence, especially north of Baghdad.

2008-05-27.title: Optimism Grows as Marines Push Against Taliban
2008-05-27.abstract: A recent four-day operation by American Marines has bolstered confidence in NATO forces in Afghanistan.

2008-05-28.title: Al Qaeda Warrior Uses Internet to Rally Women
2008-05-28.abstract: From her home in Brussels, Malika El Aroud has become one of the most prominent Internet jihadists in Europe.

2008-05-28.title: Parents’ Grief Turns to Rage at Chinese Officials
2008-05-28.abstract: Protests by grieving parents are forcing Chinese officials to address a growing political backlash over shoddy construction of public schools.

2008-05-28.title: Fearing Floods, Chinese Order Evacuations in Quake Area
2008-05-28.abstract: The Chinese authorities are evacuating 150,000 people threatened by possible flooding, if natural dams on a major river were to burst.

2008-05-28.title: China Leader Makes Debut in Great Wall of Facebook
2008-05-28.abstract: A supporter of Chinese Prime Minister Wen Jiabao has set up a page on the popular social networking Web site. Already, the page has drawn more than 12,500 supporters.

2008-05-28.title: Mexico: 7 Federal Police Officers Killed in Shootout With Drug Cartel
2008-05-28.abstract: The officers were the latest victims in a conflict between the cartels and the police that has claimed the lives of more than 30 federal agents and 170 local police officers in the last 18 months.

2008-05-29.title: Human Rights Report Assails U.S.
2008-05-29.abstract: Amnesty International again urged the United States to close the Guantánamo Bay detention camp.

2008-05-29.title: France: Sarkozy Pulls Down European Labor Rules
2008-05-29.abstract: President Nicolas Sarkozy announced that France would open its borders on July 1 to all workers from eight former Communist countries that joined the European Union four years ago.

2008-05-29.title: British Leader Warns of Global Oil Price Shock
2008-05-29.abstract: Prime Minister Gordon Brown of Britain made the comments as protests against the soaring cost of fuel threatened to widen in Europe.

2008-05-29.title: Rival to Iran’s President Is Elected Speaker
2008-05-29.abstract: The move was a strong signal of growing impatience with President Mahmoud Ahmadinejad’s economic policies.

2008-05-29.title: Britain Joins a Draft Treaty on Cluster Munitions
2008-05-29.abstract: The sudden shift by Prime Minister Gordon Brown created fresh pressures on the United States, which had counted Britain as one of its staunchest allies in opposing the ban.

2008-05-30.title: South Africa Plans Shelters for Foreigners Who Fled Attacks
2008-05-30.abstract: South Africa decided to set up temporary shelters rather than large refugee camps for foreigners driven from their homes in xenophobic violence near Johannesburg and Cape Town.

2008-05-30.title: Tough Questions for China on Schools and Japan Aid
2008-05-30.abstract: Chinese officials expressed concerns about aid from Japanese military aircraft, while they faced criticism that their poor oversight might have contributed to the collapse of schools during the earthquake.

2008-05-30.title: Wildlife Areas in Balkans to Expand
2008-05-30.abstract: The governments of Albania, Bosnia, Croatia, Montenegro, Serbia and Slovenia agreed to increase cross-border conservation efforts.

2008-05-30.title: Stonehenge Used as Cemetery From the Beginning
2008-05-30.abstract: With the help of radiocarbon dating, scientists may now have solved at least part of the mystery of Stonehenge.

2008-05-30.title: NATO Chief in Afghanistan Says Pakistan’s Tack on Militants Is Not as Expected
2008-05-30.abstract: Gen. Dan K. McNeill voiced concern on Thursday that Pakistan had not tackled militancy on its border and had recently stopped its cooperation with NATO on border issues.

2008-05-31.title: Gunman Kills 8 Worshipers at Mosque in Yemen
2008-05-31.abstract: In recent weeks an escalating war between northern rebels and the Yemeni military has spread southward, but it was not clear whether the mosque attack was related to the rebellion.

2008-05-31.title: Growing Opposition to Iraq Security Pact
2008-05-31.abstract: Prime Minister Nuri Kamal al-Maliki is trying to negotiate a security agreement that will set out how long American forces will stay in Iraq, and under what rules they will operate.

2008-05-31.title: Former EADS Chief Faces Preliminary Charges
2008-05-31.abstract: French judges have filed preliminary charges of insider trading against Noël Forgeard, the former co-chief executive of European Aeronautic Defense and Space, his lawyer said.

2008-05-31.title: Putin Maintains Presidential Air in Paris Trip
2008-05-31.abstract: On Vladimir V. Putin’s first trip abroad as Russia’s prime minister, he spoke for Russia as if he still ran it, which most analysts believe he still does.

2008-05-31.title: Monks Succeed in Cyclone Relief as Junta Falters
2008-05-31.abstract: The Burmese are growing closer to Buddhist monks, who are providing the aid that the generals do not.
